codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex flagged two MISSED_CRITICAL items and one OBSERVATION_ONLY item. The two MISSED_CRITICAL items concern branch list completeness in specification logic (omission of branch 8 from the codex_required=true and codex-disabled N/A enumerations at lines 209 and 210). These are content-correctness observations about protocol specification accuracy, not style standard violations under any of the 11 defined style standards. The OBSERVATION_ONLY item correctly confirmed that the line 45/46 omissions are pre-existing, consistent with the inspector's own pre-diff analysis.

findings.issue: commands/close.md:209 omits branch 8 from the codex_required=true verdict branch list. Branch 8 (QA-side ambiguity fallback) applies when codex is enabled.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'verdict branches 1/2/3/4/5/6/7 with branch 9 N/A' with 'verdict branches 1/2/3/4/5/6/7/8 with branch 9 N/A'.
findings.rationale: Branch list completeness in a protocol specification is a content-correctness issue, not a style standard violation. None of the 11 style standards (no-inline-code, no-hardcoded-values, use-source-venv, integer-step-numbering, meaningful-naming, english-only, merge-similar-scripts, concise-documentation, parameterized-scripts, dangling-references, merge-conflicts) govern specification logic accuracy or protocol enumeration completeness. The finding is substantively interesting but is out-of-scope for the style inspector mandate. Logged for downstream awareness.

findings.issue: commands/close.md:210 says 'Branches 3/6/7 are N/A in this mode' for codex-disabled path, omitting branch 8 which is also N/A when codex was never invoked.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace 'Branches 3/6/7 are N/A in this mode.' with 'Branches 3/6/7/8 are N/A in this mode.'
findings.rationale: Same rationale as the previous finding: specification logic completeness is out-of-scope for the style inspector. Note also that line 210 was in-scope for this diff (the change from 'branch 7' to 'branch 9' was made here), so the branch 8 omission is technically on a changed line. However, the omission itself is not a style standard violation under any of the 11 defined standards. Logged for downstream awareness.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY from codex: line 45/46 pre-existing omissions also miss branch 8, not only branches 4 and 5. Consistent with the inspector's pre-diff analysis.
findings.rationale: Confirms pre-existing status. Not newly introduced by this diff. Consistent with inspector's own analysis.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both MISSED_CRITICAL findings reclassified as observation_only: they are content-correctness observations about specification branch enumeration, outside the style-inspector's 11-standard mandate. No violations added to the violations list as a result. All three findings logged in findings[] for downstream awareness.
